question_text: What is the final sign of a "Sovereign" man in the Belgian Social Arena?
historical_context: The "Sovereign" man is the "Dual Guardian" of his community’s standards.

options.text: He is the "Anchor of Sanity"—a man whose "Presence" ensures that "Rational Order," "Conviviality," and "Institutional Trust" are maintained.
options.motivation: This is the peak. You have mastered Phase 3.

options.text: He is the most powerful man in the "Verenigingsleven."
options.motivation: Power is "External." Sovereignty is "Internal Quality."

options.text: He never has to apologize because he is always right.
options.motivation: This is "Arrogant." A "Master" knows when to use "Social Lubrication" to fix a "Friction."

question_text: You are introduced to a female peer at a networking event. What is the most appropriate physical greeting?
historical_context: In the Belgian middle class, the handshake is the "First Seal." While "bises" are common among friends, the professional arena demands a "Neutral" starting point to ensure mutual respect.

options.text: A firm, professional handshake with direct eye contact.
options.motivation: This is the "Standard of Reliability." It establishes you as an equal in the "Public Arena" and reinforces your "Professional Branding."

options.text: Offering two or three "air kisses" (the Belgian 'bises') immediately.
options.motivation: This is too informal for a first-time "Common" contact. It oversteps the "Physical Boundary" before "Social Lubrication" has occurred.

options.text: A polite wave from a distance without physical contact.
options.motivation: This can be seen as "Distant" or "Cold," potentially damaging the "Social Fabric" of the introduction.

question_text: You are waiting for coffee with a female colleague you don't know well.
historical_context: The "Coffee Machine" is a "Semi-Public" space. Middle-class etiquette requires "Adaptive Linguistics"—being friendly without being "Noisy" about personal details.

options.text: Discuss a recent high-quality cultural event or a neutral observation about the office infrastructure.
options.motivation: This is "Safe Navigation." It demonstrates "Cultural Enrichment" and keeps the conversation in the "Public Arena" without being intrusive.

options.text: Ask her how much she pays for childcare and which school her children attend.
options.motivation: This is a "Private Arena" intrusion. It violates the "Sanctity of the Private" too early in the relationship.

options.text: Stay completely silent and look at your phone.
options.motivation: This is seen as "Socially Awkward." In the "Social Arena," a small amount of "Social Lubrication" is expected to maintain "Conviviality."

question_text: You are attending an "After-work Drink" where clients will be present.
historical_context: For women, the "Professional Spiegel" is about "Stability." Your attire communicates your "Internal Quality" before you even speak.

options.text: Wear a full formal suit to ensure you are taken seriously.
options.motivation: This might be too "Stiff" for a social setting, creating a "Physical Friction" that makes you appear unapproachable.

options.text: Wear a "Smart Casual" ensemble—quality trousers or a skirt with a professional blouse and subtle accessories.
options.motivation: This is the "Polished Mirror." It shows "Verzorgd" (well-groomed) discipline while allowing for "Conviviality."

options.text: Wear very casual weekend clothes to show you are "relaxed."
options.motivation: This is "Unpolished." It suggests a lack of "Professional Branding" and can be seen as a "Class Shift" error.

question_text: You are having lunch with three female colleagues of equal rank. The bill arrives.
historical_context: Modern Belgian etiquette for women emphasizes "Autonomy." Splitting the bill is the "Standard" for maintaining "Institutional Trust" among peers.

options.text: Suggest splitting the bill equally among everyone.
options.motivation: This reinforces the "Equality of Peers" and maintains "Rational Order." It prevents "Status" friction regarding money.

options.text: Insist on paying for everyone to show how successful you are.
options.motivation: This is a "Money" and "Status" error. It can make others feel "Submissive" or uncomfortable, damaging the "Social Fabric."

options.text: Wait for the oldest person at the table to offer to pay.
options.motivation: This relies on an outdated "Hierarchy." In the modern middle class, "Reliability" means being ready to pay your share.

question_text: You are speaking to a female client who is significantly older than you.
historical_context: The transition from "U" to "Je" is a "Silent Agreement." Overstepping this boundary is a common "Class Shift" error.

options.text: Use "U" (formal) until she explicitly invites you to use "Je" (informal) or uses it first.
options.motivation: This shows "Administrative Patience" and respect for the "Social Hierarchy." It protects your "Professional Branding."

options.text: Use "Je" immediately to create a "warm" and "friendly" atmosphere.
options.motivation: This is "Unpolished." It can be seen as a lack of respect for the "Regional Nuance" of age and experience.

options.text: Avoid using any pronouns at all to stay safe.
options.motivation: This makes the conversation feel "Stilted" and "Stiff," preventing real "Conviviality."

question_text: A peer says, "That is a very elegant coat you are wearing."
historical_context: Compliments are a form of "Social Lubrication." Accepting them with "Polished Modesty" reinforces your "Internal Quality."

options.text: "Thanks! It was on sale at a discount outlet."
options.motivation: While modest, focusing on the "Discount" can sometimes devalue the "Quality" image you are projecting.

options.text: "Thank you, I appreciate that. I’ve found it to be very practical for this weather as well."
options.motivation: This acknowledges the compliment with "Dignity" and justifies the possession through "Logic" and "Quality."

options.text: "I know, it’s from an incredibly expensive designer."
options.motivation: This is "Boasting" and a "Money" error. it violates "Material Modesty" and creates "Social Friction."

question_text: You are at a social lunch with colleagues and your phone sits on the table.
historical_context: Full attention is the "Currency of Respect" in the Belgian middle class. Constant phone usage is seen as "Low-Status" behavior.

options.text: Keep the phone in your bag or face down and ignored.
options.motivation: This shows "Presence" and respect for the "Physical Arena." It confirms that the people you are with are your priority.

options.text: Check it every time a notification lights up.
options.motivation: This is "Unpolished." It signals that "External Noise" is more important than the "Social Fabric" of the lunch.

options.text: Narrate what you are doing: "Just checking a message from my babysitter, one second."
options.motivation: While it provides a "Rational" reason, it still disrupts the "Synchronized Order" of the conversation.

question_text: You are speaking to someone, and another person interrupts to ask a quick question.
historical_context: A "Notable" woman manages the "Arena" by not allowing the "Mosaic" of conversation to be shattered by "Intrusion."

options.text: Ignore the interrupter until you have finished your sentence.

options.text: Offer a warm, firm handshake, make direct eye contact, and say: "It’s a pleasure to meet you. I’ve heard great things about the 'Rational Order' of your department."
options.motivation: This establishes "Professional Branding." You show respect without being "Submissive," and your use of "Merit-based" praise shows you understand the "Public Arena."

options.text: Give her a quick double-kiss on the cheeks to show you are "friendly" and "modern."
options.motivation: This is a "Physical Boundary" error. At Level 1, especially with a senior, "Administrative Distance" is required until "Trust" is built.

options.text: Stand back and wait for her to initiate the greeting while looking at the floor.
options.motivation: This is "Submissive." It suggests a lack of "Internal Quality" and makes you appear "Socially Awkward" rather than "Reliable."

question_text: Two peers are discussing a colleague’s "Private Arena" (her recent breakup) while you are waiting for the microwave.
historical_context: The Belgian middle class values "Discretion." A young woman who can "Navigate" around gossip without being a "Killjoy" is seen as a future "Guardian of Sanity."

options.text: Join in so you don't seem "Stiff" or "Arrogant."
options.motivation: This is "Low-Status" behavior. Engaging in "Gossip Pollutants" tarnishes your "Professional Spiegel" and marks you as "Unreliable."

options.text: Offer a neutral, "Rational" distraction: "I haven't heard about that. By the way, have you seen the new 'Administrative' updates on the company portal? They look very efficient."
options.motivation: This is "Social Navigation." You protect the "Sanctity of the Private" for others and redirect the "Arena" back to "Merit" and "Order."

options.text: Leave the room immediately to show you are above such talk.
options.motivation: This is "Cold." It creates "Social Friction" with your peers. A "Master" stays and subtly shifts the "Register" instead.

question_text: You have a meeting with a traditional client. The office code is "Casual," but you want to ensure "Institutional Trust."
historical_context: "Verzorgd" is a core Belgian value. For young women, your appearance is the "First Seal" that proves you are a "Stable" representative of your institution.

options.text: Wear your most fashionable, trendy outfit to show you are "Creative" and "Young."
options.motivation: This can be seen as "External Noise." It may distract from your "Merit" and cause the client to doubt your "Stability."

options.text: Choose a "Verzorgd" (well-groomed) look: a blazer over a simple top, tailored trousers, and clean, high-quality shoes.
options.motivation: This is the "Polished Mirror." It projects "Rational Order" and "Professional Branding," signaling that you take the "Arena" seriously.

options.text: Wear a very oversized hoodie and sneakers because "that's who I am."
options.motivation: This is a "Class Shift" error. It lacks "Respect for the Craft" of the meeting and suggests you are "Unpolished."

question_text: You have an important point to make, but two male colleagues keep talking over you.
historical_context: The "Public Arena" in Belgium is a place of "Synchronized Order." A woman who uses "Rational" language to command attention is respected more than one who uses volume.

options.text: Wait for a small pause, lean in slightly, and say: "I have a 'Rational' addition to that point that clarifies the 'Logic' we discussed earlier."
options.motivation: This is "Adaptive Linguistics." You use "Soft Power" and "Professional Branding" to reclaim the "Arena" without creating "Public Friction."

options.text: Shout over them to show you are a "Strong Woman."
options.motivation: This is "Unpolished." It adds "Noise" to the room and suggests you have lost "Emotional Sovereignty."

options.text: Stay silent and send your point in an email later.
options.motivation: This is "Passive." It fails the "Proactive Reliability" test. You must be able to "Navigate" the "Physical Arena" in real-time.

question_text: You are out with colleagues on a Friday night. One of them takes a photo of you holding a cocktail and wants to tag you.
historical_context: For the 19–30 generation, the "Digital/Physical Boundary" is porous. A "Reliable" woman ensures her online "Mirror" always reflects "Internal Quality."

options.text: "Sure, but let’s make sure the 'Professional Spiegel' stays intact—maybe a photo of the whole group 'Convivially' talking instead?"
options.motivation: This is "Mastering the Boundary." You participate in the "Social Lubrication" but protect your "Digital Ledger" from "Unpolished" imagery.

options.text: Untag yourself immediately the next morning and don't say anything.

question_text: You are at lunch with three other young female professionals. The bill arrives and there is no "Notable" senior present.
historical_context: Modern Belgian women prioritize "Financial Sovereignty." Being ready to pay your share is a sign of "Betrouwbaarheid" (reliability).

options.text: Try to calculate exactly what you ate to save 2 Euros.

question_text: You are speaking to a female supervisor who is 10 years older than you. She hasn't told you to use "Je" yet.
historical_context: The "Linguistic Frontier" between formal and informal is a major test of "Social Navigation" in Belgium. For young women, staying "Proper" is always the safer "Anchor."

options.text: Continue using "U" and a formal "Register" until she invites you to do otherwise.
options.motivation: This shows "Respect for the Hierarchy" and "Administrative Patience." It protects your "Professional Branding" from being "Unpolished."

options.text: Start using "Je" immediately to show you are "Modern" and "Equals."
options.motivation: This is "Arrogant" (Dikke Nek). It violates the "Silent Agreement" of Belgian social levels.

options.text: Avoid using any names or pronouns to avoid the choice.
options.motivation: This makes the conversation "Stiff" and prevents "Social Lubrication."

question_text: You need a document urgently, and the administrative assistant is currently busy with another task.
historical_context: In Belgium, every role has its "Dignity." A "Notable" woman in training treats everyone with "Conviviality," recognizing that the "Administrative" foundation is what holds the "Institution" together.

options.text: "I see you're managing a 'Rational Order' there. When you have a moment of 'Administrative' space, could you help me with this file? It’s for the [Project] 'Merit'."
options.motivation: This shows "Respect for the Craft." You acknowledge her work and provide a "Rational" reason for your request, maintaining "Institutional Trust."

options.text: Stand over her desk until she looks up and notices you.
options.motivation: This is "Intrusive" and creates "Physical Friction." It lacks "Conviviality."

options.text: Interrupt her and say your task is more important because you are a "Manager."

question_text: What is the primary indicator of success for a young woman at Level 1 of the Social Arena?
historical_context: The Belgian middle class values "Stability." A young woman who provides an "Anchor of Sanity" is the one who will be trusted with the "Sovereign" tasks of the future.

options.text: Being "Betrouwbaar" (Reliable)—someone who is seen as a "Safe Anchor" because of her "Rational Order" and "Convivial" respect for others.
options.motivation: This is the foundation of "Institutional Trust."

options.text: Being the most popular person in the office.
options.motivation: Popularity is "External Noise." Reliability is "Internal Quality."

options.text: Getting a promotion within the first six months.
options.motivation: This focuses on "Status" rather than "Foundation." In Belgium, "Merit" must be proven through "Time" and "Stability."

question_text: At a networking event, a female peer (of similar age) loudly criticizes your outfit or appearance under the guise of "helping you fit in."
historical_context: In the Belgian middle class, personal remarks in public are seen as "Unpolished." A young woman who stays calm proves she is an "Anchor of Sanity" compared to the "Noisy" peer.

options.motivation: You use "Adaptive Linguistics" to reclaim your "Autonomy." By pivoting the conversation, you signal that her "External Noise" has no power over your "Stability."

options.motivation: This creates "Public Friction." A "Master" of the arena corrects behavior by being "Immutable," not by attacking.

question_text: The team is celebrating a success at a cocktail bar. The environment is becoming very informal, and people are ordering strong drinks.

question_text: You are in a meeting where a group of peers is arguing loudly and the "Social Fabric" is tearing.

question_text: U spreekt voor het eerst een nieuwe Belgische collega aan. Welk aanspreekvorm is correct?

options.text: Begin met 'jij/je' — informeel is vriendelijker en moderner.

question_text: U zit aan tafel bij een formeel Belgisch diner. Hoe houdt u uw handen korrect?
historical_context: In België worden tafelmanieren gezien als de 'lakmoesproef' van iemands opvoeding. De handplaatsing boven tafel met polsen op de rand is een fundamentele norm die in alle sociale lagen wordt verwacht.

options.text: Handen altijd boven tafel, polsen rustend op de rand — ellebogen van tafel.
options.motivation: Dit is de Belgische norm, aangeleerd van jongs af. Het toont 'opvoeding' en respect voor de tafel en de gastheer.

options.text: Handen op schoot — dit geeft een ontspannen en informele uitstraling.
options.motivation: In België worden handen op schoot snel opgemerkt als een teken van slechte opvoeding. Dit is een veelgemaakte fout van niet-Belgen.

options.text: Ellebogen op tafel leunen voor een ontspannen, betrokken houding.
options.motivation: Ellebogen op tafel zijn in formele Belgische settings niet aanvaardbaar. Polsen zijn goed; ellebogen niet.

question_text: U wordt brood geserveerd aan tafel tijdens een Belgisch diner. Hoe handelt u correct?
historical_context: Het broodprotocol is een van de meest herkenbare etiquette-punten aan de Belgische tafel. Snijden van brood met een mes is een teken van slechte tafelmanieren in formele settings.

options.motivation: Dit is de correcte Belgische broodetiquette. Elk stukje wordt apart gebroken en individueel gesmeerd — niet het hele sneetje tegelijk.

options.motivation: Beter dan snijden, maar in formele settings is het de bedoeling om kleine stukjes per keer te breken en te smeren.

question_text: U verlaat de tafel tijdelijk tijdens een Belgisch diner. Waar legt u uw servet?
historical_context: De servetplaatsing is een subtiel maar duidelijk signaal aan de gastheer en de bediening. Links van het bord betekent 'ik kom terug'; op de stoel is in formele settings onelegant.

options.text: Op de stoel — zo raakt het niet in de weg.

question_text: U werkt voor het eerst samen met Vlaamse collega's in Antwerpen. Welke communicatiestijl sluit het best aan?

options.text: Warm, uitbundig en expressief — toon meteen uw enthousiasme.
options.motivation: Vlamingen waarderen eerlijkheid en directheid, maar overdaad aan emotie of expressiviteit wekt argwaan.

question_text: U legt een eerste contact met Waalse zakenpartners in Luik. Wat is de grootste culturele valkuil?

options.text: Te snel zakelijk worden zonder eerst een persoonlijke band op te bouwen.
options.motivation: In Wallonië bouwt men eerst een persoonlijke relatie op voordat men ter zake komt. Overslaan van dit relationele proces wordt als koud en respectloos ervaren.

options.text: Te informeel zijn en voornamen gebruiken bij de eerste ontmoeting.
options.motivation: Dit is ook een risico, maar minder bepalend dan het ontbreken van relationele opbouw. Titels zijn in Wallonië inderdaad gebruikelijker.

options.motivation: Hoewel Frans de voorkeurstaal is in Wallonië, weigeren Walen zelden te communiceren in een andere taal als de wil er is. Het gebrek aan relationele investering is de grotere valkuil.

question_text: U bent aanwezig op een eerste professionele vergadering in Brussel. Wat onderscheidt Brusselse etiquette van de andere regio's?

options.text: Meertaligheid en het schakelen tussen talen zijn de norm — oogcontact en herhalen van de naam zijn cruciaal.
options.motivation: In Brussel is men gewend aan een internationale context. Moeiteloos schakelen tussen talen en diplomatisch formuleren zijn de kernvaardigheden.

options.text: Brusselaars zijn het meest formeel — gebruik altijd titels en wacht op het initiatief van de gastheer.
options.motivation: Formaliteit is aanwezig, maar in Brussel is het meer gebonden aan professionele status dan aan traditie. Dit is een versimpeling.

options.text: Brussel volgt dezelfde regels als Vlaanderen — begin gewoon in het Nederlands.
options.motivation: Brussel is geen Vlaamse stad. Nederlands starten kan als taalnationalisme overkomen. Linguïstische neutraliteit is de standaard.

question_text: U werkt samen met iemand uit de Duitstalige Gemeenschap in de Oostkantons. Wat is een cruciaal verschil met de andere Belgische regio's?
historical_context: De Duitstalige Gemeenschap combineert Belgische hartelijkheid met een meer Germaanse discipline. Punctualiteit is hier heiliger dan in de rest van België, en formaliteit in aanspreekvorm is de norm.

options.text: Men is informeler dan in Brussel — voornamen zijn snel de norm.
options.motivation: Het tegendeel is waar. De Duitstalige Gemeenschap is formeler qua aanspreekvorm: men gebruikt altijd de achternaam tot expliciet anders gevraagd.

options.text: Er is geen significant verschil met Vlaamse etiquette.
options.motivation: Er zijn overeenkomsten, maar de Germaanse invloed op discipline, punctualiteit en formaliteit maakt de Duitstalige Gemeenschap duidelijk onderscheidend.

question_text: A Flemish CEO says: "Good to see you again, [First Name]."

options.text: Respond using their Surname and "U" until they explicitly say "Call me Jan."
options.motivation: This is the etiquette-correct choice in the Belgian professional context.

options.text: Use the same informal approach as in the Netherlands — Belgium is similar enough.
options.motivation: Belgian and Dutch etiquette diverge significantly. Assuming equivalence is a common and costly mistake.

options.text: Default to English to avoid any protocol missteps.
options.motivation: Reasonable in international Brussels contexts, but not always the most culturally attuned response.

question_text: You are at a wedding in Namur. A lady approaches you.

options.text: Wait for her to initiate. Be prepared for three air-kisses if she is a close friend of the family.
options.motivation: This is the etiquette-correct choice in the Belgian professional context.

options.text: Use the same informal approach as in the Netherlands — Belgium is similar enough.
options.motivation: Belgian and Dutch etiquette diverge significantly. Assuming equivalence is a common and costly mistake.

options.text: Default to English to avoid any protocol missteps.
options.motivation: Reasonable in international Brussels contexts, but not always the most culturally attuned response.

question_text: You are introduced to a Baron at a gala.

options.text: Bow your head slightly during the handshake and address him as "Monsieur le Baron."
options.motivation: This is the etiquette-correct choice in the Belgian professional context.

options.text: Use the same informal approach as in the Netherlands — Belgium is similar enough.
options.motivation: Belgian and Dutch etiquette diverge significantly. Assuming equivalence is a common and costly mistake.

options.text: Default to English to avoid any protocol missteps.
options.motivation: Reasonable in international Brussels contexts, but not always the most culturally attuned response.

question_text: You are in an elevator with a neighbor you've seen once.

options.text: A polite nod and "Goiedag/Bonjour" is mandatory. Silence is acceptable, but ignoring the presence is not.
options.motivation: This is the etiquette-correct choice in the Belgian professional context.

options.text: Use the same informal approach as in the Netherlands — Belgium is similar enough.
options.motivation: Belgian and Dutch etiquette diverge significantly. Assuming equivalence is a common and costly mistake.

options.text: Default to English to avoid any protocol missteps.
options.motivation: Reasonable in international Brussels contexts, but not always the most culturally attuned response.

question_text: Een Belgische collega zegt niets na uw voorstel en laat een lange stilte vallen. Wat betekent dit het meest waarschijnlijk?
historical_context: De Belgische stilte als communicatiemiddel is cultureel uniek. Een niet-Belg die aandringt na een stilte, overtreedt een ongeschreven regel.

options.text: Hij stemt in — in België betekent stilte instemming.
options.motivation: Dit is een veelgemaakte misinterpretatie. Belgische stilte is bijna nooit instemming.

options.text: Hij denkt nog na. Dring niet aan — de beslissing komt later, via een derde of per mail.
options.motivation: Belgen, vooral Vlamingen, gebruiken stilte strategisch als teken van serieuze overweging, niet als instemming of weigering.

options.text: Hij is beleefd aan het weigeren — Belgen zeggen nooit rechtstreeks nee.
options.motivation: Hoewel Belgen soms indirect zijn, is stilte niet automatisch een beleefde weigering. Context en relatie bepalen de betekenis.

question_text: Een Belgische zakenrelatie nodigt u na twee jaar uit voor een zakenlunch. U denkt dat jullie nu vrienden zijn. Is dat zo?
historical_context: In België is een thuisbezoek een significante sociale promotie, geen vanzelfsprekendheid. Dit onderscheid is cultureel diepgeworteld en verwarrend voor buitenlanders.

options.text: Ja — een uitnodiging na twee jaar bevestigt vriendschap in België.
options.motivation: Dit is een veelgemaakte fout. In België onderscheidt men streng tussen 'kennis' en 'vriend'. Een zakenlunch maakt u geen vriend.

options.text: Nee — een echte vriendschap blijkt pas wanneer u thuis wordt uitgenodigd en aan de partner wordt voorgesteld.
options.motivation: In België wordt vriendschap nooit hardop benoemd, maar blijkt uit concrete daden: uitgenodigd worden thuis is de drempel.

options.text: Waarschijnlijk wel, maar het is beleefd om dit niet expliciet te benoemen.
options.motivation: Bescheiden, maar onjuist. De Belgische drempel voor echte vriendschap ligt veel hoger dan in veel andere culturen.

question_text: U bent op een Belgisch feestje met zowel Vlamingen als Walen. Welk type humor is sociaal het veiligst?
historical_context: Zelfspot en droge ironie zijn bindmiddel in Belgische sociale kringen. Een buitenstaander die dit mist, wordt als 'te serieus' of 'te Nederlands' beschouwd.

options.text: Grappen over de communautaire spanningen tussen Vlamingen en Walen — dit breekt het ijs.
options.motivation: Dit is het enige absolute taboe in gemengd Belgisch gezelschap. Grappen over de taaldiscussie worden zelden goed ontvangen.

options.text: Milde zelfspot en droge ironie — en aansluiten bij Belgische grappen over België zelf.
options.motivation: Belgen lachen graag om zichzelf en hun land. Zelfspot is een bindmiddel in Belgische sociale kringen.

options.text: Geen humor gebruiken — de Belgen zijn te formeel voor grappen.
options.motivation: Belgen waarderen humor zeker, maar droge ironie en zelfspot zijn de sleutel — niet openlijk formalisme.

question_text: U bent gastheer bij een zakelijk diner in België. Wanneer bespreekt u zakelijke onderwerpen?
historical_context: In hogere kringen betaalt de gastheer stilzwijgend, zonder zichtbare transactie. Het betwisten van de rekening is een sociale faux-pas.

options.text: Direct bij aankomst — efficiëntie is in België een teken van respect.
options.motivation: In de Belgische zakencultuur wordt business pas na het hoofdgerecht besproken. Direct beginnen wordt als ongepast beschouwd.

options.text: Na het hoofdgerecht — de maaltijd zelf is de relatieopbouwfase.
options.motivation: Bij een zakelijk diner in België is het protocol: eerst genieten, daarna zaken. Het initiatief om te betalen ligt bij de uitnodiger.

options.text: Wanneer de wijn is ingeschonken — dat is het informele startsignaal.
options.motivation: Dit varieert per context, maar in formele Belgische settings is na het hoofdgerecht de geaccepteerde norm.

question_text: Vous commencez une collaboration avec des partenaires wallons à Liège. Comment construisez-vous la relation professionnelle ?
historical_context: En Wallonie, la relation personnelle précède toujours la relation d'affaires. Les dîners durent plus longtemps et sont plus chaleureux qu'en Flandre. L'utilisation des titres (Monsieur/Madame) reste plus répandue qu'au nord du pays.

options.text: Passez directement à l'agenda professionnel — l'efficacité est une marque de respect.
options.motivation: En Wallonie, aborder les affaires sans avoir établi un rapport personnel préalable est perçu comme froid et irrespectueux. C'est une erreur fréquente des non-Belges.

options.text: Construisez d'abord un lien personnel lors de repas informels avant d'aborder les sujets professionnels.
options.motivation: L'hospitalité est une valeur centrale de la culture wallonne. Bâtir une relation via des interactions sociales est une condition préalable, pas une option.

options.text: Envoyez une lettre formelle avec votre proposition avant de vous rencontrer.
options.motivation: Cela témoigne d'un respect formel, mais manque de la chaleur personnelle attendue dans la culture d'affaires wallonne. Le contact direct est préférable.

question_text: Vous arrivez à une réception professionnelle à Namur. Comment saluez-vous une personne que vous connaissez peu ?
historical_context: En Wallonie, la 'bise' (les baisers sur la joue) s'impose plus rapidement qu'en Flandre. La tradition des trois baisers (gauche-droite-gauche) est plus répandue dans les cercles wallons et bruxellois traditionnels.

options.text: Une poignée de main ferme suffit — c'est professionnel et neutre.
options.motivation: Acceptable dans un contexte très formel, mais en Wallonie la 'bise' s'impose rapidement même en contexte professionnel. Rester à la poignée de main peut paraître froid.

options.text: Attendez son initiative pour la 'bise' : un ou trois baisers selon les usages locaux.
options.motivation: Laisser l'initiative à l'autre personne est la règle d'or. En Wallonie, trois baisers (gauche-droite-gauche) est la norme pour les personnes que l'on connaît bien.

options.text: Embrassez directement — c'est la manière chaleureuse de se saluer en Wallonie.
options.motivation: Initier une 'bise' sans attendre le signal de l'autre personne est une violation de la règle de l'initiative. Cela peut créer de la gêne, même en Wallonie.

question_text: Vous êtes invité à dîner chez des Wallons. Que devez-vous apporter comme cadeau ?
historical_context: En Belgique, les cadeaux sont rarement ouverts immédiatement en présence du donateur. Dans les milieux plus aisés, une lettre de remerciement manuscrite dans les 48 heures est attendue, pas optionnelle.

options.text: Des chrysanthèmes — des fleurs élégantes et durables.
options.motivation: Les chrysanthèmes sont en Belgique le symbole du deuil. Les offrir est une faute grave de protocole, quelle que soit la région.

options.text: Un bon vin ou des chocolats belges de qualité.
options.motivation: Ce sont les choix classiques et sûrs. Évitez les nombres impairs de fleurs (association avec le deuil) et tout ce qui porte ostensiblement les logos de marques luxueuses.

options.text: Rien — l'invitation elle-même est déjà un honneur suffisant.
options.motivation: Dans certains milieux informels c'est acceptable, mais une invitation à dîner en Wallonie est une promotion sociale significative. Arriver les mains vides peut décevoir l'hôte.

question_text: You are at a professional reception in Brussels. You do not know your counterpart's language preference. How do you open the conversation?
historical_context: Brussels is officially bilingual (French/Dutch) but is predominantly French-speaking in practice, with a large international community. Language mistakes are rarely taken as offence, but language awareness is deeply appreciated.

options.text: Open in English or with a bilingual greeting ('Bonjour/Goeidag') and let them signal their preferred language.
options.motivation: Linguistic neutrality is the Brussels professional standard. A neutral or bilingual opening allows the other person to choose without awkwardness.

options.text: Open in Dutch — you are in Belgium and Dutch is an official language.
options.motivation: Technically correct but can feel presumptuous. Brussels is majority French-speaking in practice; defaulting to Dutch may come across as tone-deaf.

options.text: Ask directly: 'Do you speak French or Dutch?' before saying anything else.
options.motivation: Too abrupt as an opener. A neutral bilingual greeting achieves the same goal more diplomatically.
